input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.content:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.

data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-24T02:29:40.960Z</current_datetime>

My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to MSTest v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute which has been removed. Let me look at their test file to understand the current code and provide the fix.

data.content:
MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsException<T>()` which existed since v2).

Here are the fixes:

toolCalls.name: edit

data.content:
**What changed:** Replaced `[ExpectedException(typeof(T))]` with `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>(() => ...)` inside each test body.

**Why:** MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]` because it couldn't pinpoint *which* line should throw. `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` wraps only the specific call you expect to throw, making tests more precise. It also returns the exception object so you can assert on its message/properties if needed.

> **Tip:** If you want to match derived exception types too, use `Assert.Throws<T>()` instead.
